question: Will the U.S. invade Cuba in 2026?
narrative_context: Trump says 'Cuba is next' target after Venezuela (March 28, 2026)
Pentagon official reveals 'Operation Total Extermination' regime change effort (March 23, 2026)
Secretary of State Rubio explicitly declares objective is 'regime change' (March 2026)
U.S. warships positioned in northern Cuba; Florida Strait naval ops active (March 2026)
Cuba military officially preparing for potential US military aggression (March 22, 2026)
Trump admin conducting severe oil blockade on Cuba with tariff threats (March 2026)
evidence: Trump administration statement: 'Cuba is next' military target after Venezuela (Al Jazeera, March 28, 2026)
Pentagon official: 'Attacks in Latin America are just the beginning' amid 'Operation Total Extermination' (The Intercept, March 23, 2026)
Secretary of State Marco Rubio: US objective is 'regime change' in Cuba (State Dept, March 2026)
U.S. warships positioned in northern Cuba; active naval operations in Florida Strait and Bahamas (Pentagon reports, March 2026)
Cuba military preparation: 'ready for any potential US attack' post-blackout (Al Jazeera, March 22, 2026)
Historical precedent: U.S. conducted rapid military intervention in Venezuela (Jan 2026), leading to Maduro's capture
FIU (Florida International University) analyst assessment: U.S. 'will have no choice but to engage in military operation in Cuba' (March 2026)
counter_evidence: Diplomatic talks between US and Cuban officials are ongoing; General Francis Donovan (SOUTHCOM head) told lawmakers troops are NOT actively rehearsing invasion; 'invasion' semantically distinct from airstrikes/special ops; full invasion requires Congressional declaration (politically difficult with midterms 8 months away) and 6-12 month logistics.
thesis: Trump administration rhetoric on Cuba has escalated sharply post-Venezuela success. Explicit regime-change objective, military positioning, oil blockade, and Pentagon statements about continued 'Latin American operations' all signal elevated invasion risk. However, market prices only 23.5% probability—implying high confidence in status quo despite Pentagon's 'total extermination' language. The gap between rhetoric (regime-change objective stated) and military readiness (no active invasion prep per SOUTHCOM) creates uncertainty. We estimate 45% probability, accounting for: (1) Trump's demonstrated willingness to use force (Venezuela precedent), (2) Pentagon's explicit language, (3) Secretary of State's regime-change objective, (4) active military positioning vs. (5) Congressional and diplomatic barriers, and (6) semantic distinction between invasion vs. limited military operations. Market's 23.5% appears to underweight demonstrated administration intent and precedent.
x_thread: Trump admin is preparing to invade Cuba. They explicitly said 'Cuba is next' (March 28). Secretary of State Rubio: regime change objective. Pentagon: 'Operation Total Extermination.' Market prices 23.5% invasion risk. We estimate 45%. 21.5pp gap.

The evidence: US warships positioned in northern Cuba. Active naval ops in Florida Strait. Cuba military mobilized. Pentagon said Latin American attacks 'are just the beginning.' But here's the counter: diplomatic talks ongoing, no active invasion rehearsals yet, logistics take months.
